campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including a report on how the city's investments performed that month and a detailed breakdown of the treasurer's accounts. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, including buses, biking, and walking routes.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and money management
City treasurer's financial report for July
Multimodal transportation improvements (buses, bikes, pedestrian access)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new local law) and skipping the usual first reading step to speed up the process. They directed staff to put it on the consent calendar so it can be officially adopted at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing conducted to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked (first reading waived)
Ordinance scheduled for adoption at next regular meeting
Specific topic of the ordinance not detailed in agenda

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was mostly procedural housekeeping items to officially record previous meeting discussions and move forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approval of multiple March meeting minutes from various council sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council reviewed updates on programs helping young people in the city, including youth empowerment initiatives and summer safety programs. The council also heard reports on code enforcement activities and plans to improve library facilities and services for customers.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and summer programs for kids and teens
Code Enforcement Annual Report
Library facility upgrades and customer experience improvements
Youth Intervention Services performance review

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 13, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on April 13, 2026 to approve previous meeting minutes and hear from residents during public comment. The council discussed upcoming city events and held smaller subcommittee meetings to dive deeper into specific topics.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting records
Public input and resident comments
Upcoming special events in Mountain View
Subcommittee breakout discussions on specific issues

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their connections to Sunnyvale. The group also approved the official notes from their previous March meeting.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission meeting and flag ceremony
Personal stories shared by three commissioners about Sunnyvale
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held a workshop to teach residents how to use OpenGov, a tool that shows where the city spends its money and makes the budget more transparent. The council also reviewed rules about who needs to register as a lobbyist if they try to influence city decisions.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool training for residents
City budget review and public engagement
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city ordinance
Rules for disclosing attempts to influence city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 13, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a special committee meeting focused on economic development and marketing for the city. The meeting was held in person with remote participation options available so residents could join either way.
keyTopics: Economic Development & Marketing Committee (Ad Hoc) meeting
In-person and remote participation options via Zoom
Public comment submission process (email by 11 AM meeting day)
City efforts to attract business and promote Santa Clara

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several planning and administrative matters. Main items included updating the city's 5-year housing and community development plan, reviewing requests from council members for new projects, and potentially changing the rules for how council vacancies are filled.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to council vacancy procedures
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a proposal to upgrade the historic status of the building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking to reclassify it from a lower historic protection level to a higher one, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space in exchange for renovating the building and making it more earthquake-safe.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Downtown Commercial zone development incentive
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule approval
